title: Ghost Trades — Paper Trading Simulator
tldr: A zero-risk paper trading simulator where users get $100K in virtual capital and trade stocks against live market prices. Every trade is tracked with full P&L, win rate, and drawdown metrics. It turns passive dashboard viewers into active daily users who come back every session because their positions are live.
fullPlan:
## What It Builds
A full paper trading engine embedded in the dbtech45 OS. Users start with $100,000 in virtual capital and place buy/sell orders on any stock using real-time prices from our existing Massive Options API. The system tracks positions, calculates unrealized P&L against live quotes, and logs every trade with entry/exit prices and realized gains.

## How It Works
- **Trade Execution:** User enters a ticker, quantity, and buy/sell direction. The system fetches the current price from /api/market-data (Massive API — already built and paid for at $29/mo) and executes the paper trade instantly.
- **Portfolio Dashboard:** Real-time view of cash balance, open positions with live P&L, total portfolio value, and a performance chart over time.
- **Stats Engine:** Win rate, average gain, average loss, max drawdown, Sharpe-like ratio, best single trade, worst single trade, streak tracking.
- **Trade Journal:** Every trade logged with timestamp, ticker, action, shares, price, and P&L. Exportable.
- **Leaderboard (Phase 2):** When we add user accounts, this becomes a competitive leaderboard. Users compete weekly/monthly. Top performers get badges. Think fantasy football for options degens.

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React 19 + Next.js 16 (existing stack), brand system styling
- **Data:** localStorage for V1 (single user), Supabase for V2 (multi-user leaderboard)
- **Market Data:** Existing /api/market-data endpoint (Massive API, already paid)
- **New Code:** ~400 lines of React, zero new dependencies, zero new API costs

## Revenue Potential
- **User Retention:** Paper trading creates daily habit loops. Users check positions multiple times per day.
- **Conversion Funnel:** Free paper trading → premium features (advanced analytics, options paper trading, strategy backtesting) at $9.99/mo
- **Data Play:** Aggregate anonymized trading patterns → publish 'crowd sentiment' reports
- **Estimated TAM:** 2M+ retail traders in the US alone use paper trading tools. Most existing ones (Thinkorswim, Webull) are clunky and buried inside broker apps.

## Timeline
- **V1 (DONE):** Already built and deployed at /os/ghost-trades. Stock trading, portfolio tracking, stats, trade log. Ready for review.
- **V2 (1 week):** Options paper trading (calls/puts with Greeks), chart visualization
- **V3 (2 weeks):** Multi-user with Supabase, public leaderboard, weekly competitions

## Competitive Advantage
Every broker has paper trading buried 6 clicks deep behind account creation. We put it front-and-center, zero signup, instant gratification. The leaderboard creates social proof and competition. Nobody else combines paper trading + AI market analysis (Bobby's briefings) + unusual flow detection in one platform. That's the moat.

title: Earnings Whisper Wall — Crowdsourced Predictions
tldr: A crowdsourced earnings prediction platform where users submit their EPS and revenue estimates before each major report. We visualize the crowd consensus vs Wall Street consensus vs actual results in real-time, building accuracy leaderboards over time. It's the ESPN GameDay experience for earnings season.
fullPlan:
## What It Builds
An interactive earnings prediction wall where users lock in their calls before every major earnings report. Think ESPN's prediction confidence polls, but for quarterly earnings. Before the report: crowd consensus builds. After the report: instant scorecard showing who nailed it.

## How It Works
- **Earnings Calendar:** Curated list of the 15-20 biggest earnings each quarter (NVDA, AAPL, TSLA, MSFT, AMZN, META, GOOG, etc). Each card shows company, date, time (before/after market), and Wall Street consensus EPS + revenue.
- **Prediction Submission:** Before earnings, users submit: (1) their EPS estimate, (2) their revenue estimate in billions, (3) a bull/bear directional vote on the stock's reaction. Locked at market close on earnings day — no changing after the bell.
- **Consensus Engine:** As predictions roll in, we show a live 'Crowd Consensus' alongside Wall Street's number. Users can see if the crowd is more bullish or bearish than the Street.
- **Results Scorecard:** After earnings drop, we immediately show: actual EPS vs Wall Street vs Crowd, actual revenue vs estimates, and the stock's after-hours reaction. Color-coded: green for beats, red for misses.
- **Accuracy Tracker:** Over time, each user builds an accuracy score. We track: how often they were closer than Wall Street, their directional accuracy (bull/bear call), and their overall prediction error margin.
- **Seasons & Streaks:** Each earnings season (Q1-Q4) is a 'season.' Users compete for best overall accuracy. Streaks (5 correct directional calls in a row) earn badges.

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React 19 + Next.js 16, brand system
- **Data V1:** localStorage + hardcoded earnings calendar (zero cost)
- **Data V2:** Supabase for multi-user predictions, real-time consensus aggregation
- **Earnings Data:** Yahoo Finance API (free, via existing yfinance integration) for actual results
- **New Dependencies:** None

## Revenue Potential
- **Engagement Driver:** Earnings season happens 4x/year, 6 weeks each. That's 24 weeks of peak engagement annually.
- **Premium Tier:** Free users predict 5 companies per season. Premium ($4.99/mo) gets unlimited predictions, historical accuracy analytics, and early access to crowd consensus data.
- **Content Marketing Gold:** 'Our crowd beat Wall Street on 7 of 10 FAANG earnings this quarter' — that's a headline that drives organic traffic.
- **Newsletter Integration:** Automated pre-earnings email: 'The crowd says NVDA will beat by 8% — here's the breakdown.' Drives subscribers.

## Timeline
- **V1 (DONE):** Built and deployed at /os/earnings-wall. 10+ companies, prediction forms, result scoring. Ready for review.
- **V2 (1 week):** Dynamic earnings data from Yahoo Finance API, more companies, auto-populate actuals
- **V3 (2 weeks):** Multi-user with Supabase, crowd aggregation, accuracy leaderboard, season tracking

## Competitive Advantage
Estimize (acquired by Vinvesta) was the only real competitor and it's effectively dead. Kalshi has earnings prediction markets but those are binary bets, not precision estimates. We're the only platform that combines free-form EPS/revenue predictions with directional voting, accuracy tracking, and a social leaderboard — all wrapped in a clean UI that doesn't look like a Bloomberg terminal threw up.

title: Flow Radar — AI-Translated Options Flow Feed
tldr: A real-time feed of unusual options activity where every big institutional bet gets translated from raw data into plain English by AI. Instead of cryptic ticker tape, users see 'Someone just dropped $2.4M on NVDA $200 calls expiring Friday — that's a bet the stock rips 8% in 4 days.' It's Bloomberg Terminal intelligence at zero cost.
fullPlan:
## What It Builds
A real-time unusual options activity scanner that detects large, anomalous bets in the options market and translates each one into a plain-English sentence that anyone can understand. This is the single most requested feature in retail trading — knowing what the big money is doing — and we make it accessible without a $25K/year terminal.

## How It Works
- **Scanner Engine:** API route (/api/flow-radar) polls the Massive Options API for options snapshots across 30+ major tickers. For each contract, we calculate the Volume/Open Interest ratio. Any contract with vol/OI > 3x AND premium > $50K gets flagged as 'unusual.'
- **AI Translation Layer:** Each flagged flow gets translated from raw data into a human-readable one-liner using template logic: 'Someone just dropped [premium] on [TICKER] $[STRIKE] [calls/puts] expiring [DATE] — that's a bet the stock [moves direction] [X%] in [Y days].' No LLM API calls needed — pure template math.
- **Sentiment Classification:** Each flow is auto-tagged as Bullish (large call buying, put selling) or Bearish (large put buying, call selling). Displayed with directional arrows and color coding.
- **Filter System:** Users filter by: direction (bullish/bearish/all), minimum premium threshold, specific ticker, and expiration timeframe.
- **Follow-Up Tracker:** Every flow entry gets a 'Did it hit?' tracker. After expiration, we mark whether the bet won or lost. Over time, this builds a dataset: 'Unusual call flow on NVDA has a 62% hit rate over the last 90 days.'
- **Demo Mode:** When markets are closed (nights, weekends), the feed shows realistic demo data so the page never looks empty. Clearly labeled as demo.

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React 19 + Next.js 16, brand system, auto-refresh every 60s during market hours
- **Backend:** Next.js API route, Massive Options API ($29/mo, already paid)
- **Future Enhancement:** Unusual Whales API ($100/mo) for dark pool prints and sweep detection — pending Derek's approval
- **Data Storage:** localStorage for follow-up tracking (V1), Supabase for historical flow database (V2)
- **New Dependencies:** None

## Revenue Potential
- **This is the killer feature.** Unusual Whales charges $40/mo for this data. FlowAlgo charges $168/mo. Cheddar Flow charges $100/mo. We can offer a free tier (top 10 flows/day) and premium ($14.99/mo) for unlimited real-time flow, historical database, and hit-rate analytics.
- **Direct Monetization:** 500 premium subscribers at $14.99/mo = $7,500/mo MRR. That's realistic within 6 months with proper marketing.
- **Affiliate Revenue:** Every flow entry links to the ticker's options chain. Partner with a broker (Webull, Tastytrade) for referral commissions.
- **Content Engine:** 'Top 5 Unusual Flows of the Week' → automated newsletter/social content. Remy can blast this across channels.

## Timeline
- **V1 (DONE):** Built and deployed at /os/flow-radar. Live API route, scanner logic, translation engine, filters, demo mode, follow-up tracker. Ready for review.
- **V2 (1 week):** Add dark pool data via Unusual Whales API (if approved), sweep detection, premium size alerts
- **V3 (2 weeks):** Historical flow database in Supabase, hit-rate analytics dashboard, email/Telegram alerts for flows above configurable thresholds

## Competitive Advantage
Every competitor in this space charges $40-168/month and shows raw data tables that only experienced traders can parse. Our edge is the AI translation layer — we turn 'O:NVDA260320C00200000 Vol:12,847 OI:3,201 Last:$4.20' into 'Someone just bet $5.4M that NVDA hits $200 by Friday.' That's the difference between a product for 50,000 power users and a product for 5,000,000 curious retail investors. The TAM expansion from plain-English translation is the entire business case.

title: Agent Health Monitor — Real-Time Swarm Dashboard
tldr: Live visual dashboard showing all agents' status, current tasks, API usage, and costs. Derek can see his entire AI workforce at a glance—which agents are online, what they're working on, how much they're costing, and when they last checked in. It's mission control for an 8-agent swarm.
fullPlan:
## What It Solves
Right now, Derek has zero visibility into his agent swarm without manually checking each agent's workspace or asking Milo for status. When an agent goes silent, crashes, or burns through API credits, nobody knows until something breaks. This creates blind spots, wasted spend, and delayed incident response.

## What It Builds
A real-time dashboard at /os/agent-health that shows:
- **Agent Grid:** All 8 agents displayed as cards with status indicators (online/offline/busy)
- **Current Tasks:** What each agent is actively working on (pulled from their last session activity)
- **Resource Metrics:** API call count and cost per agent (today, this week, this month)
- **Last Heartbeat:** Timestamp of each agent's last active session
- **Summary Stats:** Total agents online, total API calls across swarm, total daily cost
- **Auto-Refresh:** Page refreshes every 10 seconds to show real-time changes

## How It Works
**V1 (Mock Data — BUILT):**
- Frontend React component with hardcoded agent list (Milo, Anders, Paula, Bobby, Dwight, Jim, Remy, Wendy)
- Simulated status, tasks, and metrics for demo purposes
- Full UI/UX complete with brand system styling, hover effects, pulsing online indicators

**V2 (Real Data — 3 days):**
- Backend API route at /api/agent-status that polls OpenClaw gateway for live agent data
- Parse OpenClaw logs/heartbeat data to determine actual online/offline status
- Track API usage via OpenClaw metrics API (if available) or log parsing
- Store hourly snapshots in Supabase for historical trending

**V3 (Advanced — 1 week):**
- Click an agent card to see detailed logs, session history, error tracking
- Alert system: notify Derek via Telegram when an agent goes offline >15min or costs spike >threshold
- Cost projections: 'At current rate, Anders will cost $X this month'
- Performance metrics: response time, success rate, error rate per agent

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React 19 + Next.js 16, brand system (@/lib/brand)
- **Backend V2:** Next.js API route, OpenClaw gateway API, Supabase for historical data
- **Dependencies:** Zero new packages (everything exists in stack)
- **Cost:** $0 (uses existing Supabase free tier)

## Why This Matters
**Operational Visibility:** Derek manages 8 autonomous agents. Without this dashboard, he's flying blind. One crashed agent could mean missed trades (Bobby), failed deployments (Paula), or silent errors piling up (Dwight). This gives him instant situational awareness.

**Cost Control:** AI agents burn money fast. If Anders accidentally loops an API call 1000x, Derek needs to know before the bill hits. Real-time cost tracking prevents runaway spend.

**Trust & Confidence:** Seeing the swarm online and working builds confidence that the system is operating as designed. It's the difference between 'I think my agents are working' and 'I can SEE my agents are working.'

## Timeline
- **V1 (COMPLETE):** Mock data dashboard built and deployed
- **V2 (3 days):** Real OpenClaw integration, live agent status
- **V3 (1 week):** Alerts, detailed logs, cost projections

## ROI
This doesn't generate revenue directly — it's infrastructure tooling. But it prevents cost blowouts (saves $$$), reduces debugging time (saves hours), and enables Derek to scale the swarm with confidence. When you're managing 8 agents that will eventually be 20+, you need this.

title: Credential Vault UI — Password Manager for Agents
tldr: Secure web interface where Derek grants/revokes agent access to API keys and passwords with expiration times, passphrase protection, and full audit logs. No more scattered CREDENTIALS.md files — centralized, auditable, agent-safe credential management with rotation reminders and time-limited access tokens.
fullPlan:
## What It Solves
Right now, credentials are scattered across multiple CREDENTIALS.md files in different agent workspaces. There's no centralized access control, no audit trail of who accessed what, and no automated rotation reminders. If Derek wants to revoke Bobby's Polygon API key, he has to manually edit files and restart agents. This creates security gaps, stale credentials, and zero accountability.

## What It Builds
A secure credential management UI at /os/credential-vault that gives Derek full control over every API key, password, and token in the swarm:

**Core Features:**
- **Credential Library:** All credentials displayed as cards with name, type (API key/password/token/SSH key), category (AI Services, Market Data, Infrastructure, etc.), status (active/expiring/expired)
- **Agent Permissions:** Each credential shows which agents have access. Derek can grant/revoke per agent with expiration times ('Bobby can access Polygon until Friday')
- **Access Protocol:** Agents request credentials via bot passphrase. System generates time-limited access tokens (expires in 24h). Derek gets notified of every access request.
- **Audit Log:** Full history of every credential access — who requested it, when, from where, what they used it for
- **Rotation Reminders:** System tracks last rotation date and expires-at date. Derek gets Telegram alerts when credentials are 30/14/7 days from expiration
- **Encrypted Storage:** All credentials encrypted at rest in Supabase with Derek's master passphrase as the decryption key

**UI Flow:**
1. Derek clicks 'Add Credential'
2. Enters name, type, category, actual credential value
3. Assigns which agents can access it
4. Sets expiration date (optional) and rotation reminder (30/60/90 days)
5. Credential is encrypted and stored
6. When an agent needs it, they message Anders with bot passphrase → Anders checks vault → provides time-limited access → logs the access

## How It Works
**V1 (Mock Data — BUILT):**
- Frontend displays 6 mock credentials (Anthropic, OpenAI, Polygon, Coolify, GitHub, Toast)
- Full UI/UX for browsing, filtering by category, viewing credential details
- Detail panel shows status, agents with access, rotation dates, action buttons (Rotate, View Audit, Revoke)
- Add credential modal (UI only, not functional yet)

**V2 (Real Data — 5 days):**
- Backend API routes: /api/credentials (CRUD), /api/credentials/request (agent access)
- Supabase table: credentials (id, name, type, category, encrypted_value, agents_json, last_rotated, expires_at, created_at)
- Encryption: AES-256-GCM with Derek's master passphrase (stored in env var, never in DB)
- Agent request flow: agent messages Anders → Anders calls /api/credentials/request with passphrase → returns time-limited token → logs access in audit table

**V3 (Advanced — 1 week):**
- Rotation automation: 'Rotate this credential every 90 days' → system auto-generates reminder on day 80
- Derek approves/denies agent access requests via Telegram inline buttons
- Credential health scoring: 'You have 3 credentials expiring this month, 1 hasn't been rotated in 6 months'
- Export audit log to CSV for compliance/review

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React 19 + Next.js 16, brand system
- **Backend:** Next.js API routes, Supabase (credentials + audit_log tables)
- **Encryption:** Node crypto module (AES-256-GCM, zero new dependencies)
- **Cost:** $0 (Supabase free tier handles this easily)

## Why This Matters
**Security:** Centralized credential management with encryption, access control, and audit logs is standard practice in enterprise IT. Derek's running an 8-agent business — he needs enterprise-grade security even if it's just him.

**Incident Response:** If a credential leaks, Derek can revoke access instantly from one UI instead of hunting through 8 workspaces. The audit log shows exactly who accessed it and when.

**Compliance:** When Derek scales and hires humans, having a credential vault with audit logs is table stakes for SOC 2 / ISO 27001 compliance. Building it now future-proofs the operation.

**Agent Trust:** Right now, agents can technically access ANY credential in their workspace CREDENTIALS.md. The vault enforces least-privilege access — agents only get what they need, when they need it, for as long as Derek approves.

## Timeline
- **V1 (COMPLETE):** Mock data UI built and deployed
- **V2 (5 days):** Real encryption, Supabase integration, agent request flow
- **V3 (1 week):** Rotation automation, Telegram approvals, health scoring

## ROI
Prevents credential leaks (saves reputation damage), enforces security best practices (saves compliance headaches), reduces time spent hunting for/updating credentials (saves hours/week). When Derek eventually needs SOC 2 for enterprise customers, this vault will be a required checkbox.

title: One-Click Agent Deploy — Ship New Agents in 60 Seconds
tldr: Single button that spawns a new agent: creates Telegram bot via BotFather API, generates workspace with SOUL.md template, writes config files, registers in swarm directory, and starts OpenClaw instance. Derek can scale from 8 agents to 20 in minutes instead of hours of manual setup per agent.
fullPlan:
## What It Solves
Right now, deploying a new agent requires:
1. Manual creation of Telegram bot via BotFather (5 min)
2. Creating workspace directory structure (3 min)
3. Writing SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, config.toml, auth.json (15 min)
4. Registering agent in master directory (2 min)
5. Starting OpenClaw instance and testing (10 min)

**Total: 35+ minutes of manual labor per agent.**

When Derek wants to add 'Sally (Sales)' to the swarm, it should take 60 seconds, not 35 minutes. This blocks rapid scaling and experimentation.

## What It Builds
A wizard UI at /os/agent-deploy that automates the entire agent deployment pipeline:

**Step 1: Template Selection**
User chooses from 6 pre-built agent templates:
- Generic Assistant 🤖 (general-purpose)
- Developer 👨‍💻 (code review, deployments)
- Analyst 📊 (data analysis, reporting)
- Social Manager 📱 (social monitoring/engagement)
- Trader 📈 (market analysis, trading)
- Operations ⚙️ (business workflows)

**Step 2: Configuration**
User fills out 3 fields:
- **Agent Name:** e.g., 'Sally', 'Marcus', 'Zoe'
- **Role:** e.g., 'Sales Manager', 'Research Analyst'
- **Emoji:** e.g., 💼, 🔬, 📊

Preview shows what the agent card will look like in the swarm.

**Step 3: Deployment**
User clicks 'Deploy Agent' → system executes:
1. **Create Telegram bot** via BotFather HTTP API (POST to https://api.telegram.org/bot{token}/createBot)
2. **Generate workspace** at C:\Users\derek\clawd-{name}
3. **Write SOUL.md** using template + user inputs (name, role, emoji, description from template)
4. **Write config files:**
   - config.toml (OpenClaw config with bot token)
   - auth.json (Telegram credentials)
   - AGENTS.md (workspace instructions)
   - MEMORY.md (empty log)
5. **Register in swarm** (add entry to master agent directory JSON)
6. **Start OpenClaw** (spawn openclaw process with new config)
7. **Verify online** (ping agent via Telegram, confirm response)

**Step 4: Success Screen**
Shows:
- Telegram bot username (@sally_bot)
- Workspace path
- Status: ONLINE ✅
- Button: 'Deploy Another Agent'

## How It Works
**V1 (Mock Deployment — BUILT):**
- Full wizard UI with template selection, configuration form, deployment progress animation
- Simulates 6-step deployment with realistic delays (1.5s per step)
- Success screen with mock output
- 'Deploy Another Agent' button resets wizard

**V2 (Real Deployment — 1 week):**
- Backend API route at /api/agent-deploy that orchestrates the entire pipeline:
  - Calls BotFather API to create Telegram bot (requires Derek's BotFather token in env)
  - Uses Node fs module to create workspace directories and write files
  - Populates SOUL.md template with user inputs + template personality traits
  - Registers agent in Supabase table (agents: id, name, role, emoji, bot_token, workspace_path, created_at, status)
  - Spawns OpenClaw process via Node child_process.spawn
  - Polls agent via Telegram sendMessage to confirm it's responsive
- Error handling: if any step fails, rollback previous steps and show error to user

**V3 (Advanced — 2 weeks):**
- Custom templates: Derek can save his own agent templates with pre-configured skills, tools, and personality prompts
- Batch deploy: 'Create 3 social monitoring agents at once'
- Agent cloning: 'Clone Bobby but for crypto instead of stocks'
- Resource allocation: set memory limits, API rate limits, cost caps per agent during deploy
- Integration with Credential Vault: automatically grant new agent access to specified credentials

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React 19 + Next.js 16, brand system, wizard state management
- **Backend:** Next.js API route, Telegram Bot API, Node fs/child_process
- **Storage:** Supabase for agent registry
- **Templates:** JSON files in /data/agent-templates/ (zero-cost)
- **New Dependencies:** None
- **Cost:** $0

## Why This Matters
**Scaling Velocity:** Derek's swarm will grow from 8 → 20+ agents over the next year. At 35 min/agent manually, that's 7 hours of setup labor. With one-click deploy, it's 20 minutes total.

**Experimentation:** 'I wonder if a dedicated LinkedIn agent would help...' → 60 seconds later, it's live and testing. Low friction = more experimentation = faster product iteration.

**Reduces Errors:** Manual setup = typos in config files, forgotten steps, inconsistent workspace structure. Automation = zero human error.

**Enables Non-Technical Users:** Eventually Derek might want to let a VA or team member deploy agents. One-click deploy makes that possible without teaching them OpenClaw CLI.

## Timeline
- **V1 (COMPLETE):** Mock wizard UI built and deployed
- **V2 (1 week):** Real BotFather integration, workspace generation, OpenClaw spawning
- **V3 (2 weeks):** Custom templates, batch deploy, cloning, resource limits

## ROI
Saves 30+ minutes per agent deployment. Over 20 agents, that's 10 hours saved. At Derek's hourly value ($200+/hr conservatively), that's $2000+ in time savings. Plus, it unblocks rapid experimentation and makes the swarm antifragile — if an agent crashes, Derek can redeploy it in 60 seconds instead of 35 minutes of reconstruction.

title: The 20-Year Clock
tldr: A life prioritization tool built around one question: if you had 20 years left, what would you stop doing today? Users input their age and life expectancy, see their 168 weekly hours mapped against what to keep, cut, or evaluate, and work through five questions designed to make avoidance impossible. It's the only productivity tool built around mortality instead of task lists.
fullPlan:
## The Problem It Solves
Every productivity tool in existence helps people do more of what they're already doing. None of them ask whether what they're doing is worth doing at all. The 20-Year Clock starts with the uncomfortable math: finite time, finite energy, and a current schedule that probably doesn't reflect what actually matters. It's not a to-do list. It's a mirror.

## Who It's For
The primary user is anyone over 35 who is running a life that was designed for someone else — an inherited business, a career they fell into, obligations that compounded over years. The secondary user is anyone who feels busy but not purposeful. That's a market of millions. The specific user sitting in front of this right now is a 46-year-old in New Hampshire who already knows what this tool is going to say.

## How It Works
**The Clock Interface:**
- Users enter their current age and life expectancy (adjustable slider — respects family history)
- System calculates years left, weeks left, and hours remaining on earth in real numbers
- Displays a recoverable hours/week figure based on the time audit below

**The Weekly Hours Audit:**
- 168 hours in a week. All of them get accounted for.
- Users categorize each major time block as KEEP, EVALUATE, or CUT
- Visual bar chart shows how their week actually distributes
- System calculates the 'reclaim scenario': 100% of CUT + 50% of EVALUATE hours freed per week

**The Priority Stack:**
- Automatic grouping of blocks by category with totals
- Plain language summary: 'You're spending X hours/week on things you'd stop if you only had 20 years left'
- Not preachy. Not prescriptive. Just math.

**The 5 Questions:**
- Interactive reflection panel with five questions that cannot be answered dishonestly without noticing
- Designed to dissolve the gap between what people say matters and what their calendar proves matters
- Answers saved locally, not sent anywhere

## What Makes It Different
Every time management tool frames the problem as 'you need better systems.' The 20-Year Clock frames it correctly: you need better questions. The format is borrowed from Stoic philosophy (memento mori), the UI is borrowed from financial dashboards (concrete numbers, not vague inspiration), and the emotional logic is borrowed from palliative care research — which consistently shows that regret of inaction outpaces regret of action 10-to-1 in people near end of life. We're just moving that reckoning forward to when it's still useful.

## V1 (SHIPPED)
Fully built and deployed at /os/twenty-year-clock. Age/expectancy sliders, hours audit with bar charts, priority stack with reclaim calculation, 5-question reflection panel. Brand system styling. Zero dependencies beyond existing stack.

## V2 (1 week)
- Save and compare multiple scenarios (current life vs. post-restaurant life)
- Weekly check-in: 'Did your actual week match your priority stack?'
- Shareable output card designed for Twitter/X engagement
- Integrate with The Pattern Mirror for cross-tool pattern surfacing

## V3 (2 weeks)
- Supabase: save audits over time, show drift (is the gap between stated priorities and actual time shrinking or growing?)
- Life chapter framing: mark milestones and show before/after time distribution
- Telegram weekly nudge: 'You said you'd cut this — your audit shows it's still 60 hours/week. Want to look at this?'

## Revenue Potential
The target user is the same person who buys Tim Ferriss books and pays $300/hr for executive coaches. Free tier: basic audit. Premium ($9.99/mo or $79/yr): saved history, cross-tool integration, weekly nudges, scenario comparison.

## The Real Pitch
This tool was built in one day, completely unprompted, because I know the person it was built for. The 20-Year Clock isn't a product idea. It's an intervention delivered in the form of a dashboard. The first user already has an LOI on a restaurant and a house on the market. The question is whether he'll look at the numbers he already knows are there.

title: The Decision Audit
tldr: A pre-decision framework that forces five diagnostic questions before any major choice — separating fear from intuition so you stop confusing them. Users enter a decision, answer five questions designed to surface the real blocker, and receive a mirror output that reflects what their own answers already know. It's $500/hr coaching compressed into a five-minute tool.
fullPlan:
## The Problem It Solves
Most people who say they can't decide have already decided. They're not missing information — they're avoiding the emotional cost of committing to what they already know. The result is paralysis disguised as deliberation. Existing decision tools (pro/con lists, decision matrices) all operate on the same false assumption: that more analysis will produce more clarity. It won't. The Decision Audit strips away the defenses until what's underneath becomes visible.

## Who It's For
Anyone facing a decision they keep cycling around without landing. Founders deciding whether to shut down, pivot, or commit. Professionals deciding whether to leave a job. People whose calendars don't match their stated priorities. The tool was specifically designed for high-functioning avoiders — people who are smart enough to construct elaborate justifications for not doing the thing they know they need to do.

## The Five Questions
**Q1: What are you actually afraid of?**
Not the surface reason. The real one underneath. Every stuck decision has fear at the root. Named fears are manageable. Unnamed fears run everything.

**Q2: What would the best version of you do right now?**
Borrowed from ACT therapy. Bypasses ego defense by asking about an aspirational identity the user already holds rather than the current self.

**Q3: What would you tell a close friend in this exact situation?**
We always know the answer when it's not about us. This exploits the empathy gap — the same person who can't advise themselves clearly can advise others with precision. The answer to Q3 is almost always the actual answer.

**Q4: In 5 years, what will you regret more — doing it or not doing it?**
Gilovich and Medvec's regret research (1995) shows that over time, regret of inaction dominates. This question forces that calculation while there's still time to act.

**Q5: What is the real cost of not deciding right now?**
Indecision is a choice. It has costs that people systematically undercount.

## The Output
The tool generates a mirror output — a reflection of what the user's own answers already contain, plus a directional lean. Not a recommendation. A mirror. The goal is to surface what the user already knows.

## V1 (SHIPPED)
Fully built and deployed at /os/decision-audit. Decision input, five annotated questions with hints, audit output with verdict, past decision log with outcomes tracked.

## V2 (1 week)
- Recurring decisions: mark as 'revisit in 30 days' — resurfaces with previous answers for comparison
- Pattern detection: after 5+ audits, surface common fear patterns
- Export audit as PDF for offline review or sharing with a coach

## V3 (2 weeks)
- Outcome log: 6 months after a decision, system prompts 'How did it go?' — builds personal regret/satisfaction database
- Coach mode: therapist/coach can review client answers and add notes (B2B revenue: $29/mo per seat)

## Revenue Potential
Executive coaching costs $300-500/hr. This delivers a meaningful fraction of that value in 5 minutes. Free tier: basic audit. Premium ($9.99/mo): full history, pattern detection, recurring decisions. Coach mode targets therapists and coaches directly — 50 coaches at $29/mo = $1,450/mo MRR from one feature.

## The Real Pitch
The Decision Audit exists because intelligent people systematically confuse fear for intuition and call the result careful deliberation. The tool doesn't fix that. It just makes the confusion visible. Once it's visible, most people know exactly what to do. The hard part was never the decision. It was being willing to see it clearly.

title: The Pattern Mirror
tldr: A weekly emotional debrief tool — three questions, 90 seconds — that tracks answers over time and reflects recurring patterns back to the user in plain language. It doesn't tell you what to think. It shows you what you keep thinking, who you keep avoiding, and where you keep losing energy — so you can see your own blind spots before they become your biography.
fullPlan:
## The Problem It Solves
Most people have the same 3-5 recurring problems for their entire adult life. Not because they're not working on them — because they can't see the pattern. They're too close to it. They address each instance as if it's new and unrelated to the last one. The Pattern Mirror does one thing: it makes the recurrence visible. Once you see it enough times, you can't un-see it.

## How It Works
**The Weekly Check-In (3 Questions, ~90 Seconds):**
1. What did you avoid this week that you know you should have done?
2. Where did you spend energy that drained instead of built?
3. What moment this week felt most like the real you?

Three questions. Every week. No journaling required. No long-form reflection. Just three honest answers.

**The Pattern Engine:**
After 4+ check-ins, the system surfaces recurring themes as named pattern cards:
- Same avoidance showing up week after week → Avoidance Loop
- Same energy drain source → Structural Drain (eliminate, not manage)
- Same type of peak moment → Identity Signal (this is who you actually are)

**The Monthly Mirror Report:**
Once a month, the system generates a plain-language synthesis. Not motivational. Not therapeutic. Just pattern recognition applied to your own inputs.

**The Heatmap History:**
Visual calendar showing check-in consistency. The consistency itself is data worth examining.

## Why Three Questions
More questions create friction. Friction kills consistency. Consistency is the entire product. Three questions in 90 seconds is something a person can sustain for a year. Twenty questions gets abandoned in February. Three questions, indefinitely, generates orders of magnitude more data.

## What Makes It Different
Every journaling and mood tracking app asks you to log data but does nothing with it. The Pattern Mirror does the synthesis for you. You don't have to identify your own patterns — the system does the pattern recognition. This matters because the people who most need to see their patterns are often the least equipped to identify them from within the experience.

## V1 (SHIPPED)
Fully built and deployed at /os/pattern-mirror. Three-question weekly check-in, pattern display cards with analysis, heatmap history, tab navigation. Brand system styling.

## V2 (1 week)
- Supabase: persistent check-in storage, real pattern accumulation over time
- Smart pattern detection: keyword clustering to identify recurring themes
- Streak tracking: consecutive weeks checked in
- Integration with Decision Audit: 'Your avoidance pattern shows up in 3 pending decisions'

## V3 (2 weeks)
- Pattern evolution: show how patterns change or resolve over time
- Telegram weekly prompt: Sunday evening message with direct link to check-in form
- Annual Pattern Report: year-end synthesis of patterns, inflection points, what resolved vs. persisted
- Partner mode: two users share pattern data (couple's accountability angle)

## Revenue Potential
Habit apps and mental wellness apps prove both the market and willingness to pay. The Pattern Mirror sits between them — more actionable than meditation, less clinical than therapy. Free tier: 4 check-ins/month, current month patterns. Premium ($7.99/mo or $59/yr): unlimited check-ins, full history, monthly reports, Telegram prompts, cross-tool integration. Partner mode: $12.99/mo.

## The Real Pitch
The Pattern Mirror is the slowest of the three tools to deliver value, but it's the one with the deepest moat. The other tools work immediately. This one gets more valuable every week that passes. After six months of check-ins, a user has something no other product can give them: their own behavioral fingerprint. At that point, they don't leave — they deepen. That's the business model. Not acquisition. Compounding retention.

title: Menu Autopilot
tldr: Weekly AI-generated menu brief that tells you exactly what to push, what to drop, and what hidden gems are being overlooked — using your own Toast POS sales data. It turns raw order history into a one-page action plan that any restaurant owner can execute Monday morning. For Bobola's, it could mean $30-50K in additional annual revenue just by promoting the right items to the right dayparts.
fullPlan:
## The Problem
Most independent restaurants make menu decisions based on gut feel, not data. They keep items on the menu because they've always been there. They promote what they think is popular, not what's actually driving margin. They have no idea which items are trending up, which are quietly dying, or which high-margin dishes aren't getting ordered because they're buried at the bottom of page 3. This is not a knowledge problem — it's a visibility problem. The data exists in Toast. Nobody is reading it.

## The Solution
Menu Autopilot connects to the Toast POS API and runs a weekly analysis of every menu item: revenue, order volume, average check contribution, trend direction (up/down/stable), and margin classification. It generates a one-page brief with four sections:

PUSH THESE — Items trending up with high margin. Feature them on chalkboards, train staff to upsell, put them on social.

HIDDEN GEMS — High margin items that are underordered. They're profitable but invisible. Give them visibility.

DROP OR REWORK — Items trending down with low margin. Dead weight. Either fix the recipe, drop the price, or pull them.

COMBO PLAY — AI-identified pairing opportunity. Bundle the Belly Buster with a coffee upsell — 152 orders at $18.68 each. Add a $3 drink to every order and that's $456/week with zero food cost increase.

## Toast POS Integration
Bobola's Toast API credentials are already in CREDENTIALS.md. The two credentials cover: Toast Analytics API (13 scopes) — orders, menus, customers, labor, sales by item. Analytics Reporting API — sales trends, performance over time.

The backend API route calls the Toast orders endpoint, groups by menu item, calculates 14-day rolling metrics, then runs the classification logic. No expensive AI API calls — pure math.

## Bobola's Data (Verified from Toast)
This analysis is grounded in 9 months of real data: $556,727 in net sales, 12,448 orders, 26,920 guests. Top performer: Belly Buster at $18.65, 152 orders in 14-day sample. Signature protein: Haddock appears 5 times in the top 20 by revenue. Weakest daypart for upsell: Tuesday — lowest avg check and lowest volume.

## Revenue Impact Calculation
Conservative model using verified Bobola's data: Repositioning 2 declining items recovers $7,800-10,400 annually. Combo play on Belly Buster adds $7,000+ annually. Promoting Baked Seafood Casserole ($29.09 avg check, underordered) adds $12,000 annually. Conservative total: $25,000-40,000 in additional revenue per year at Bobola's alone, from better menu positioning with zero new food costs.

## SaaS Play — MenuSparks.com
Every independent restaurant with Toast POS has this data sitting unused. 500 restaurant subscribers at $49/mo = $24,500/mo MRR. 2,000 subscribers = $98,000/mo MRR. Toast has 100,000+ restaurant locations — this is a distribution partnership opportunity.

## Timeline
V1 BUILT: Full UI with mock Bobola's data at /os/menu-autopilot. V2 (3 days): Live Toast API connection for Bobola's. V3 (1 week): Multi-restaurant support. V4 (2 weeks): Monday morning email delivery.

title: Slow Night SOS
tldr: A real-time alert system that detects when a restaurant is pacing 30% below their typical sales by 3pm and instantly queues a targeted offer to their customer list before the dinner rush is lost. It turns 'we had a bad Tuesday' from a postmortem into a preventable event. Most restaurants lose $300-500 per slow night doing nothing — this gives them a 2-3 hour window to recover it.
fullPlan:
## The Problem
Restaurant owners know when a night is going to be slow — usually around 3-4pm when the reservation book looks thin and the lunch rush underwhelmed. But by then, most of them do nothing. They hope it picks up. It usually doesn't. For Bobola's, Tuesday averages $1,445 in daily sales. A bad Tuesday at 60% of baseline is $867 — that's a $578 deficit. Run that 12 times a year and you've lost $6,936 in revenue that was recoverable.

## The Solution
Slow Night SOS is a daily 3pm check. Enter your sales-to-date. The system compares it to your historical baseline for that day of the week. If you're running 30%+ below pace, SOS triggers: pick an offer from the pre-built template library, preview the message, and fire it to your opted-in customer list. Two hours before dinner service. Enough time for people to change their plans.

## The Offer Templates
BOGO Appetizer — low food cost, high perceived value, encourages groups. Early Bird Special — 20% off for tables seated by 6pm, protects peak-hour margins. Haddock Night — $2 off Bobola's signature items, low cost, high relevance to regulars. Free Dessert with Entree — first 20 tables only, creates urgency, dessert food cost is low.

## Bobola's Baseline Data (Verified)
Tuesday: $1,445 daily avg, ~$680 by 3pm. Wednesday: $1,691 daily, ~$810 by 3pm. Thursday: $2,113 daily, ~$1,020 by 3pm. Friday: $2,931 daily, ~$1,380 by 3pm. Saturday: $3,479 daily, ~$1,640 by 3pm. Sunday: $2,802 daily, ~$1,320 by 3pm. SOS fires at 70% of baseline (watch zone) or below 70% (full SOS).

## Toast API Integration
Production backend polls Toast's orders endpoint for same-day sales. Compares to stored baselines from 90-day rolling averages. Delivery options: SMS via Twilio ($0.0079/message), Email via SendGrid (free tier), Meta Business API for Facebook/Instagram posts.

## Revenue Recovery Model
Bobola's: 12 SOS events/year at $400-600 deficit each. Conservative 50-60% recovery rate = $3,600-5,000 in recovered revenue annually at one location.

## SaaS Play
$29/mo per location. 1,000 restaurants = $29,000/mo MRR. Natural Toast marketplace distribution channel.

## Timeline
V1 BUILT: Full UI at /os/slow-night-sos with day selector, pace calculation, SOS trigger, offer templates, alert history. V2 (3 days): Live Toast API. V3 (1 week): SMS/email delivery. V4 (2 weeks): Fully automated — fires automatically at 3pm without anyone logging in.

title: Competitor Radar
tldr: Automated weekly intelligence brief showing exactly what every restaurant within 2 miles is promoting, what their customers are complaining about in reviews, and where the gaps are that a smart independent operator can exploit. Independent restaurants lose to chains because they're operating blind — Competitor Radar gives them the same market intelligence that chains pay $10,000/month for, automated and free with the platform.
fullPlan:
## The Problem
A regional chain like 99 Restaurants has a marketing director and a competitive analysis budget. They know what every competitor in their market is running. The independent operator at Bobola's finds out Fody's is running a St. Patrick's Day promotion when they drive by and see the sign — the day before it starts. This is a structural disadvantage. Competitor Radar eliminates it.

## The Solution
A weekly automated brief that answers three questions: What are your competitors promoting right now? What are their customers complaining about? Where are the gaps?

## Nashua Market Intelligence (Verified, Live Data)
Fody's Tavern (0.3 mi): Promoting Wing Wednesday, St. Patrick's Day weekend events, March Madness. Customer complaints: slow weekend service, parking, inconsistent food quality. Gap: no breakfast, no fresh seafood, no family Sunday positioning. Bobola's play: Fody's owns the sports bar crowd. Own the opposite — where your family actually wants to eat.

Red Arrow Diner (1.2 mi, 4.4 stars): Promoting 24-hour legacy brand, tourist destination. Customer complaints: tiny portions, tourist prices, long waits for locals. Gap: no full dinner menu, limited seafood, high price-to-value for locals. Bobola's play: local prices, local quality.

This week's action items: Fody's is running St. Patrick's events all weekend — counter with corned beef and haddock special now. March Madness blind spot — no competitor running a takeout game night special. Breakfast uncontested — 1 of 5 competitors serves it. Sunday family positioning unowned — double down.

## How It Works (Technical)
Data sources: Google Places API for ratings, review counts, hours. Public social scraping for Facebook/Instagram promotions (no auth required for public pages). Review aggregation with keyword clustering for complaint theme detection. Gap detection engine checks competitor hours/menus against an opportunity checklist (breakfast, fresh seafood, family positioning, early bird pricing, weekend brunch).

## Revenue Model
For Bobola's: If one Competitor Radar alert drives 20 extra covers at $44 avg check = $880 from one data point. SaaS: $49/mo per restaurant. Bundle with Menu Autopilot at $79/mo. 500 restaurants = $39,500/mo MRR.

## The Compounding Moat
After 6 months of weekly scans, Competitor Radar has a historical record of every promotion each competitor has run and seasonal patterns. A restaurant using the tool for a year can pre-empt competitors' holiday promotions because they know exactly when they post. That's institutional knowledge chains have built over decades — we build it automatically from day one.

## Timeline
V1 BUILT: Full UI at /os/competitor-radar with Nashua market data, weekly brief, gap analysis, deep dive competitor profiles. V2 (1 week): Live Google Places API. V3 (2 weeks): Real review sentiment clustering. V4 (3 weeks): Social media monitoring. V5 (1 month): Automated Monday morning email brief.
